inline_comments.message:
Is there any specific reason to use Autowired here??
You can also use the existing constructor injection provided by lombok.

NB: a real nice (readable alternative) for this is to extract out a constant using TimeUnit for the conversion
e..g 
final static int TIME_TO_LIVE_IN_SECONDS = TimeUnit.MINUTES.toSeconds(30)l

Did my comment about using TimeUnit get lost?! (did you abandon a pev patch or did I do something wrong)
in any case you can extract out something like this final int TIME_TO_LIVE_IN_SEC = TimeUnit.MINUTES.toSeconds(30)

PERFORMANCE ISSUE?!

instead just convert the advisedCmHandles  colletion!
Yes, the @Bean annotation and the haselcast don't work with constructor injection
When it is already present, this return with the value (true or false)
Sourabh works on performance issue, it's just a temporally solution to return with a list of cm handles.
can this be 'int' instead of long so also you don't have to cast in line 51?

I know this is controversial but I think I asked before to remove this. The general rules is NOT to create string constants that dont add value ie where the names is the same as the content of the string. In such cases we prefer to use the String literal in teh code.
see rule #3 here: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS+Code+Review+Check+List

NB: for readability i would like to extract this into a well-named method

but most importantly my performance concern has been resolved :-D
are you using both? I can only see ConcurrentHashMap below but maybe I missed one...

I prefer to check a bit more to prevent future false-positives:
check that the converted YangModelCmHandle has the correct ID
Should we not just pass this in directly too? I don't think we need the static variable above.
What is the difference between passing false and true as a parameter here?
It cause a sonare issue, because here are more than two usage.
There is no difference, false means work on it, true means done, but we do not use this values.
